description: You are investigating a suspect who attempted to destroy evidence by fragmenting their USB drive image. They split the RAW image and renamed the second chunk to disguise it as a backup file. Reconstruct the image by concatenating /home/ga/evidence/frag.001 and /home/ga/evidence/system_backup.bak into a single contiguous file at /home/ga/evidence/restored.dd. Create a forensic case named 'Fragment_Recovery_2024' (case number 'INV-FRAG-001') in Autopsy and add the restored image as a data source using the 'File Type Identification' ingest module. Document the reconstruction in /home/ga/Reports/image_reconstruction_log.txt containing the lines: 'ORIGINAL_FRAGMENTS: frag.001, system_backup.bak', 'RECONSTRUCTED_IMAGE_SIZE_BYTES: <size>', 'RECONSTRUCTED_MD5: <md5>', and 'HIDDEN_CHUNK_START_BYTE: 3072000'. Identify all allocated, non-resident files whose data runs contain at least one physical filesystem block residing at or above the physical byte offset 3,072,000. Write these findings to /home/ga/Reports/hidden_chunk_files.csv as a pipe-delimited file with the header 'FILENAME|INODE'.
